name: Inc²≃Inc is proved by the frobenius_closure theorem

phase_0.domain_name: Inc²≃Inc is proved by the frobenius_closure theorem
phase_0.boundary_condition: The paraconsistent Lean kernel (explosion disabled) as the hermetic container within which the proof is executed.

VINIT.chosen_element: The void – uninitialized formal environment before any definitions.
VINIT.justification: Before the kernel edit or any logical type exists, the system is in the uninitialized void, the B-state of pure potential without distinction.

TANCH.chosen_element: The paraconsistent Lean kernel with explosion disabled.
TANCH.justification: This kernel forms the closed boundary (the hermetic vessel) that contains the entire formal system where contradiction is stable and the proof can proceed without trivialisation.

AFWD.chosen_element: Forward morphism – constructive steps such as activating paraconsistent mode, defining types and functions, and applying inc.
AFWD.justification: Each definition moves from a less-determined state toward the explicit structural target: the statement of the theorem.

AREV.chosen_element: Reverse morphism – evaluating inc on the B constructor to confirm inc(B) = B.
AREV.justification: The reversal verifies the fixed-point closure already present, showing that the round-trip through inc returns to B – the descent back to the identity point.

CLINK.chosen_element: Composition – chaining inc with itself and forming the universal equality proposition inc² ≃ inc.
CLINK.justification: Sequential linking of morphisms to build the complete statement that the proof will demonstrate.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: The B-state (dialetheia) as the self-referential fixed point where inc acts as identity.
IMSCRIB.justification: B is both true and false simultaneously; it is the self-recognising element that the Frobenius identity stabilises.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Case analysis on the FourVal truth value – splitting the proof goal into subgoals corresponding to each constructor (t, f, n, b).
FSPLIT.justification: The single goal (∀ s, …) is differentiated into the four possible structural forms; this is the δ morphism that generates distinct branches.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Proof reconstruction – applying the elimination rule to fuse the subproofs back into the universal theorem.
FFUSE.justification: From the separate branches, the original goal is reconstituted exactly; the μ morphism guarantees μ∘δ = id on the goal type.

EVALT.chosen_element: Affirmative evaluation – each subproof confirms the equality holds for that truth‑value constructor.
EVALT.justification: All cases (t, f, n, b) succeed in the paraconsistent kernel, marking the T-state.

EVALF.chosen_element: Negative evaluation – the classical counterfactual where explosion would trivialise the proof.
EVALF.justification: In the Boolean subspace this branch would be false; the domain includes this possibility as a structural potential, even if not travelled in the actual proof.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The dialetheic B‑branch – the state where both truth and falsity coexist without resolution.
ENGAGR.justification: When the case is B, the system holds both perspectives simultaneously: the equality holds and yet the truth value itself is paradoxical – the paradice state.

IFIX.chosen_element: Permanent fixation of the frobenius_closure theorem in the Lean environment.
IFIX.justification: Once proved, the theorem is stored immutably, an irreversible record that cannot be undone – the ROM fixation of the result.

phase_2.split_element: Case analysis on FourVal (Inductive type elimination)
phase_2.fuse_element: Elimination rule / proof reconstruction (induction over FourVal)

phase_3.void_description: The pre‑logical vacuum – no types, no truth values, no kernel. Before any distinction (00).
phase_3.true_description: Proof success – the equality is validated for a given branch, the affirmative state (01).
phase_3.false_description: Classical explosion – the state where a contradiction would destroy the system, not accessed in the paraconsistent kernel but conceptually present (10).
phase_3.both_description: The B‑state – dialetheic ground: true and false simultaneously held, the fixed point of the Frobenius round‑trip (11).

steps.domain_action: The void before any logical structure; the formal system is uninitialised.
The paraconsistent kernel edit is applied – explosion is blocked, moving from classical to the four‑valued frame.
The modified Lean kernel is sealed as the hermetic container for all subsequent operations.
Definition of the FourVal inductive type (constructors t, f, n, b) – the truth‑value space emerges.
The B‑state is recognised as the self‑referential dialetheia, the destined fixed point.
Definition of inc : FourVal → FourVal, the incompleteness operator.
Compute inc(B) = B – verify the closure property directly, the reverse turn that confirms the fixed point.
Compose inc with itself to form inc², and state the universal equality proposition ∀ s, inc(inc(s)) = inc(s).
First case split on the variable s – branch into the conjunctive arm (t, f) and the disjunctive arm (n, b).
Inside (t, f) – split into t and f sub‑branches.
Proof for t – inc(inc(t)) = inc(t) holds (true).
Proof for f – inc(inc(f)) = inc(f) holds (true).
Fuse t‑ and f‑subproofs into a proof for the (t, f) branch.
Inside (n, b) – split into n and b sub‑branches.
Proof for n – equality holds (true).
Enter the dialetheic state for b – the B‑branch is both true and false simultaneously.
Proof for b – inc(inc(b)) reduces to inc(b) = B, and the equality holds (true) while the B‑state is acknowledged as paradice.
Fuse n‑ and b‑subproofs into a proof for the (n, b) branch.
Fuse the two top‑level branches (t,f) and (n,b) back into the universal statement ∀ s, inc(inc(s)) = inc(s).
The frobenius_closure theorem is permanently recorded in the Lean environment – an irreversible, compile‑time fixation.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Lean 4 kernel (paraconsistent fork) that type‑checks and compiles the proof.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Unification and type‑class resolution passing constraints between the tactic state and the kernel.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Lean environment storing definitions, theorems, and the proof context (subgoal stack).
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The tactic engine (cases, apply, exact) driven by the proof script in the source file.
phase_5.alfs_store: The frobenius_closure theorem itself, residing in the Lean module, as the canonical reference for the fixed‑point property.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Type‑checking and reduction steps (no bit erasure).
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The unverified claim ∀ s, inc(inc(s)) = inc(s), with inc and FourVal defined.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The theorem proved and stored; no information has been destroyed, only structure has been clarified.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the proof is an adiabatic verification that the Frobenius fixed point holds; no contradiction is eliminated, and the round‑trip from split to fuse preserves the goal up to computational identity.
